text.content:
Red-Hood Iskra

A dark and tragic one-shot for Broken Tales, designed for a single 3–5 hour session. Players will uncover the mystery of a girl abandoned in the forest and raised by wolves. A story of revenge, betrayal, and morally testing choices.

The Hunters arrive at the village during a storm. A red-caped girl is seen atop a hill, surrounded by black wolves. The village is terrified. @Actor[Elizaveta]{Elizaveta}, a noblewoman, offers sanctuary and guidance. @Actor[GerardDubois]{Gerard Dubois}, her husband, warns of a cursed child, @Actor[RedHoodIskra]{Iskra}, who seeks vengeance.

• @Actor[RedHoodIskra]{Red-Hood Iskra}: Girl raised by wolves, powerful, not inherently evil.
  • @Actor[Elizaveta]{Elizaveta}: Noble hiding a dark truth, possibly Iskra’s mother.
  • @Actor[GerardDubois]{Gerard Dubois}: Priest and Elizaveta’s husband who condemned Iskra.
  • @Actor[Greskar]{Greskar, the Big Black Wolf}: Leader of the pack, loyal to Iskra.
  • @Actor[Anastazia]{Anastazia, the Sorrowful Spirit}: Iskra’s grandmother, a restless ghost.
  • @Actor[VincentArn]{Vincent Arn}: Captain of the guard, key adversary.
  • Villagers, wolves, guards: Minor NPCs across the village and forest.

1. The Village: Investigation, suspicion, and rising tension.
  2. The Forest: Rituals, spirits, tracks, and encounters with @Actor[BlackWolves]{black wolves}.
  3. The Showdown: Final confrontation in the mansion or forest core. Truth about Elizaveta revealed.

Players may:

  • Side with @Actor[RedHoodIskra]{Iskra} to help her seek justice.
  • Attempt to redeem her through empathy.
  • Stop her to protect innocents or uphold the Order’s will.

The ending should feel morally ambiguous. Killing her ends the threat, but continues the betrayal. Saving her may defy the Order but break the cycle.

• @Item[VoiceOfSpirits]{Voice of the Spirits}: Spiritual communication without harm.
  • @Item[WolfKiller]{Wolf-killer}: Eliminate 1 minor beast NPC on defense.
  • @Item[Sweetie]{Where are you going alone, sweetie?}: Special Gift for @Actor[Garou]{Garou the Old Wolf}.

• Gerard and Elizaveta should lie or deflect at first.
  • Push players to question their morality.
  • Iskra is a victim, not a monster—she seeks justice.
  • Use @Actor[Anastazia]{Anastazia} as a conscience or narrative twist.
  • Trigger Iskra’s second Gift when she’s cornered or betrayed.

🎼 Soundtrack

Click to play ambient tracks for key scenes.

🏘️ 02. The Durfort Village

🌲 03. The Gevaudan Forest

🪦 04. The Old Cemetery

🐺 06. Feral Showdown
